facility: University of Maryland Medical Center — Cardiology
indication: Risk-stratify recurrent PE; assess for right-heart strain.
findings: NEW right ventricular dilation with reduced RV systolic function and regional hypokinesis sparing the apex (McConnell-type pattern). Septal flattening suggesting RV pressure/volume overload. Estimated RV systolic pressure elevated. LV function preserved (EF 60%). No pericardial effusion.
impression: NEW acute right-ventricular strain — not present on the 7/6 study. Consistent with INTERMEDIATE-RISK (submassive) pulmonary embolism.
comparison: 7/6/2027 TTE showed normal RV size and function. Interval development of RV strain.
